title: Executive communication preferences
body:
Compiled preferences from the exec team:

- CEO: Bullets over prose. One page max for briefings. Hates passive voice. Wants the recommendation up front, supporting detail after.
- CFO: Numbers first. Will ignore any claim without a number attached. Prefers tables over charts.
- VP Sales: Loves narrative. Wants the customer story. Will tune out if you lead with metrics.
- General Counsel: Forward anything involving contracts, IP, or public statements. Better to over-include than under-include.

title: Board meeting protocol
body:
Standard board meeting prep cycle:

- T-7 days: deck draft to CFO and GC for review
- T-5 days: deck sent to board (Monday before a Friday meeting)
- T-2 days: pre-read questions collected from board members
- T-1 day: exec team prep session to align on answers

Materials go through the GC's assistant for distribution — never email board members directly.

Board members' known priorities:
- Shalemark Venture Partners (lead investor): growth metrics, competitive position
- Brayworth Growth: path to profitability, burn rate
- Independent directors: team health, governance

title: Common email templates
body:
Template — declining a meeting request:

'Thanks for reaching out. [CEO]'s calendar is tight through [month] — would [alternative, e.g., a 15-min call with VP X] work as a starting point? Happy to find time for a fuller conversation in [later month] if useful.'

Template — customer exec intro follow-up:

'[Name], great to connect today. A few follow-ups from our conversation: [bullets]. [Contact] on my team will reach out on [specific next step]. Looking forward to [next milestone].'

Always cc the relevant AE on customer emails so they can track the thread.
